cost_sensitivity.cost_model: Future diagnostics must reject interpretation if any month-end close-window readout only survives before fees, slippage, and spread assumptions.
cost_sensitivity.scope: Value-free migration metadata only; no execution cost estimate is produced.

cost_assumptions.scope: research planning only; no execution or tradability claim

data_assumptions.data_dependency_detail: Month-end / quarter-end last-trading-session dates are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar; no external feed required. Same low-data-dependency class as opex_pinning and the event-calendar month_end_flow card, and shares its FeatureRequest implementation.
data_assumptions.missing_label_candidates: No new label candidates are declared by this legacy card.

exclusion_rules: Derive the month-end / quarter-end flag from the ACTUAL exchange trading calendar (last available trading session in the month/quarter, accounting for holidays and EARLY_CLOSE sessions), carried as known-ahead point-in-time metadata under the _require_metadata_known_as_of guard so a holiday-shifted last session is never inferred from future data.
Do not test needs_paid_data records in V0; paid-data sourcing is RED-deferred.
Do not edit or delete the legacy Track-A card until a later retirement phase is authorized.
expected_failure_modes: A calendar-derived month-end-x-close-proximity conditioner is structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms conditioned on. The most material non-orthogonality risk is internal to the seasonality set: overlap with the event-calendar month_end_flow card and redundancy with the existing day_of_week / session-position features, NOT overlap with the dead price-action factors.

Residualize forward outcomes against the dead-mechanism factors (regime/vwap_session/bbo_tradability/liquidity_pa) AND against minutes_to_rth_close (to confirm the effect is not a generic into-the-close effect on all days). Cross-check against the event-calendar month_end_flow card to ensure the two are pre-registered as ONE pooled hypothesis, not double-counted (see FDR_BUDGET_PRIORITY_2_3.md de-duplication note).

Month-end / quarter-end last-trading-session dates are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar; no external feed required. Same low-data-dependency class as opex_pinning and the event-calendar month_end_flow card, and shares its FeatureRequest implementation.
promotion_criteria: Any future WATCH or CANDIDATE routing requires reviewer-gated evidence and must preserve EXPLORATORY promotion guards.
Any future diagnostic must pass no-lookahead, duplicate-exposure, and testability checks before interpretation.

timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_description: Month-end / quarter-end last-session membership and minutes-to-close are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar and known ahead. The only hazard is mislabeling the last trading session when a holiday or early close shifts it; a naive calendar-day rule (last day-of-month) would be wrong.
timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_level: low lookahead risk declared by the legacy Track-A card
timestamp_assumptions.post_event_knowable_features: The legacy card declares no post-event knowable feature list.

hypothesis_card.author: ALPHA_IDEA_TO_VERDICT_LOOP_V0/IVL-P01
hypothesis_card.expected_mechanism: On the known-ahead final trading sessions of a calendar month/quarter, we test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned jointly on month-end-session membership AND proximity-to-RTH-close (the rebalancing window into the close) differs from ordinary sessions. This is a falsifiable rebalancing-flow conditioning hypothesis, not a profitability claim.
falsification_criteria: Reject or requeue this migrated record if required governed features or labels are unavailable before diagnostics.
Block any later verdict if no-lookahead controls or duplicate exposure checks fail before a metric is inspected.
hypothesis_card.rationale: Index funds, target-date funds, and mandate rebalancing concentrate buy/sell flow into the month-end and quarter-end closing auction window; whether this produces a measurable conditional forward pattern in the minutes into the close is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve.
risks: Month-end / quarter-end last-session membership and minutes-to-close are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar and known ahead. The only hazard is mislabeling the last trading session when a holiday or early close shifts it; a naive calendar-day rule (last day-of-month) would be wrong.

A calendar-derived month-end-x-close-proximity conditioner is structurally independent of the realized-price/quote main effects the dead mechanisms conditioned on. The most material non-orthogonality risk is internal to the seasonality set: overlap with the event-calendar month_end_flow card and redundancy with the existing day_of_week / session-position features, NOT overlap with the dead price-action factors.

Month-end / quarter-end last-trading-session dates are deterministic from the exchange trading calendar; no external feed required. Same low-data-dependency class as opex_pinning and the event-calendar month_end_flow card, and shares its FeatureRequest implementation.

This is a falsifiable rebalancing-flow conditioning hypothesis, not a profitability claim.
